slides.heading: The hidden vault `.git`
slides.body: Git snapshots your work in a repository — a vault stored in a hidden .git folder.
bullets: .git keeps history — delete it only if you mean to discard the project ledger

rows.meaning: Tell Git to watch this folder and create the hidden .git repository vault here.
rows.note: Run it once at the root of a project.

slides.heading: Run it locally
slides.body: In the simulated terminal beside this lesson, run git init first, then use ls to inspect the workspace. You should see the repository initialize and .git appear logically in Git state.
bullets: Green output means Git accepted your command
ls should list .git once init succeeds

rows.meaning: Create the repository vault for this folder.
rows.note: This slide advances after this command succeeds.

rows.meaning: List the files and folders in the current directory.
